docket_entries.description: REPLY TO PLAINTIFFS OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER (TRANSACTION ID # 64406272) (TRANSACTION ID # 64406272) FILED BY DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Charles Odiase, Esq. 213-925-9762 for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Cindy Hamilton, Esq. 650-289-7859 of Greenberg Traurig for Defendant JP Morgan Chase Bank.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues Unruh Act claim, IIED, Bane Act. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CARLOS INFANTE, JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling and changes the words on no.2 and no.3 from having to asking. Defendants JP Morgan Chase Bank N.A. and Carlos Infante demur to all five causes of action in plaintiff Jonjo Diase's complaint; the demurrers are all overruled:

1: An Unruh Act (Civ. Code 51) claim is adequately pled; plaintiff alleges he was denied access by his bank to money on the basis of his race.

2: An intentional infliction of emotional distress claim is adequately pled; swearing at a customer, ordering him to "get the fuck out" of a bank and asking an armed policeman eject him is outrageous conduct that could cause severe emotional distress.

3: A Bane Act (Civ. Code 52.1) claim is adequately pled; ordering a customer to "get the fuck out" of a bank and asking an armed policeman eject him qualifies as a threat of violence.

4: A bank depositor is not precluded from suing his bank for conversion. (Fong v. East West Bank (2018) 19 Cal.App.5th 224, 231-33.) Morse v. Crocker National Bank (1983) 142 Cal.App.3d 228, 232 is not to the contrary; it provides that a bank is obligated to pay a depositor on demand.

5: Depriving a bank depositor of access to his funds could qualify as economic injury under Business & Professions Code 17200 and 17500.

Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling, which includes the changes the Court ordered, in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
